But.

is One

It does like a keyword analysis.
And then it comes up with like a content structure.

mean, the product doesn't do as much of that today as we want it to.

But effectively, you know, if you're a customer, you come and you sign up, we ask you to connect a couple of things, like your books, and then, or like your financials, basically, and your last year's.
Tax return.

And then after that, we have AI look at those two things and then start to generate like a, you can think of it as a questionnaire that is custom to you that we need answers to certain questions to prepare your tax return.
And so, and then we drive you through the questions with AI, right, until we have enough information to prepare the draft return.
So that's like the first, the first place where AI is used.
The second place where it's used is like more internally and it doesn't touch the users.
However, we got answers to the questions with AI or otherwise, it's to prepare the draft return.

There's like some steps, like one, one is called adjustments that usually like a human has to do, like a human goes in a spreadsheet and they make, they tweak numbers before, before putting them in something called a tax calculator.
So we try to automate as much of that as possible.
So, that,
As opposed to having a human spend time on this administrative work, it kind of gets done for them.
Then we use a tax calculator, which is just, that's normal software.
There's no AI to prepare the forms for federal and a bunch of states in the US.
Then at the end, a human will actually review the return because we feel strongly that we don't want make any mistakes.
So you can actually have a human review the return.
A human will talk to the customer about the return, right, to explain what deductions they are, answer any questions.
And then we use software to sign an e-file the return.
So the goal for us is to, like, put the humans at, like, the places where there's real value to be added.
So before the tax returns for advisory, right, that means if the customer has, hey, I'm thinking about buying this.
How should I think about it?
Or, like, this expense, should I recognize it this year or next year?
Like, those kinds of conversations are human.
The preparation is mostly...
AI and just software until the review phase, right?
And the like delivery to the customer and then normal software to just get it across the finish line.
So that's like the core of the product today.
Over time, we would like the advisory to be AI driven for simple cases.
We would love like we're experimenting right now in the review phase where we send a video.
They offer a phone call with the customer, but what they're like, they record a video.
Of talking through the deductions and then they send that to the customer.

Like eventually we would like to take as much of the experience and make it digital.
But for us, there's always, this is like an important part of the product.
Like you always have this option of getting a human involved.
This is right.

Usually they call it actually like they usually ask an email and they ask their questions.
And then it's more the CPA might be like, maybe we should talk about this.

I think that's about it.
I mean, on the advisory side, like bigger companies, but we don't do really much bigger companies.

But yeah, then we don't have a lot of text messages.
I would say mostly it's like email, schedule.

Yeah.
Yeah, I totally agree.

I think when it factors back in are some of the situations that John to be mentioned.
But then also when there's like a worry or a concern.

Yeah.

Nice.

So, nah.
So, startups mostly lose money.
Yeah.

And...
For a long time, right?

Or very little.

So there are, some things that are only relevant to startups that make it more complex.
And then some things that are relevant to startups, mostly losing money that make it more simple.
So that's one lens.
The second lens is that
And startups tend to have better books because investors want them to have good books because they use the books when a financing round happens.

Otherwise, there's not a big amount of difference.
I mean, I think once a startup, you know, starts to make more than like 10 million in revenue, like all businesses get more complex.
There's a new different form you have to fill out.
So but we don't do many of those.
Most of our startups are below.

Like, you know, those businesses will have different things.

then digital businesses, we have more digital businesses.

Like more systems that are connected to.

I don't know, Ryan?

I mean, when you think about the SMBs and just by virtue of them not being a startup, it's losing money for several years.
They tend to be viable businesses that are.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Town and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, AI capabilities, and product insights.](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX's Atlas platform demonstrated for content generation, with flexible workflows adaptable to Town's needs](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=140.0)
  - [Town's product uses AI for tax return preparation, aiming to reduce human work from 7-10 hours to 1 hour](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
  - [Content calendar and research process to be refined based on insights from Town's head of tax](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Town plans website migrations: new Framer site in August, then to Sanity CMS in October/November](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1665.0)

## Topics

### Atlas Content Generation Platform

  - [Recently launched internally, minimalist design but powerful and flexible](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
  - [Demonstrates article generation workflow: topic input → assignment brief → research → outline → draft → fact-checking → internal linking](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0)
  - [Customizable artifacts (audience personas, company context, writing guidelines) feed into content creation](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [Fact-checker flags unverified claims; can be customized (e.g., for tax-specific sources like IRS website)](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=408.0)

### Town's AI-Driven Tax Preparation Process

  - [AI analyzes client's financial documents and last year's tax return](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
  - [Generates custom questionnaire for additional info needed](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
  - [Automates adjustments typically done manually by humans](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
  - [Uses tax calculator software to prepare federal and state forms](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=790.0)
  - [Human review and advisory still crucial, especially for complex cases](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=802.0)
  - [Goal: Reduce human work from 7-10 hours to 1 hour per return](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)

### Client-CPA Interaction

  - [Primarily email-based communication (70-80%)](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
  - [Initial trust-building often requires phone/video calls](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=999.0)
  - [Frequency: 1 in 20 clients want quarterly calls; most prefer minimal interaction](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)
  - [Video summaries of tax returns reducing need for calls](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=878.0)
  - [Round-the-clock access to CPAs for urgent issues (e.g., IRS letters)](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)

### Integrations and Client Base

  - [Current: QuickBooks](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1187.0)
  - [Planned: Xero, FreshBooks, Gusto, Rippling, potentially ADP](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1153.0)
  - [Startup vs. SMB differences:](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
      - [Startups often loss-making, lowering tax complexity](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1254.0)
      - [R\&D credits more relevant for startups](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1294.0)
      - [SMBs require more proactive planning due to profitability](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1350.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Aida to complete calibration blogs by Monday for Town's feedback on tone and content](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)
  - [Expand content calendar and refine topic clusters based on head of tax insights](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [Schedule additional team interviews for next week](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1586.0)
  - [Town to review content early next week](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1604.0)
  - [Address blog visibility issue on Town's Framer website](https://fathom.video/share/5AxksWpdtcEpsX51MACoyYd3vh7h1Brr?tab=summary&timestamp=1639.0)
